masthead.dek: Fifty questions on mobs, blocks, enchantments, and game logic — built to be reasoned out, not stat-memorized. Why does the gold pickaxe out-mine netherite? Which mob can be killed by its own attack? Narrow the set, place your bet, then tap to check.
masthead.footer_html: Fifty questions verified to minecraft.wiki, leaning on stable Java Edition behavior. Difficulty comes from game logic and slicers, not exact health/damage values.

dimensions: makes a creeper drop a disc
plan.note: Reasoning hooks over rote stats: 'the only that...' slicers, reflex-defeaters (gold mines fastest, rain hurts endermen), and cross-clue identification (TNT-proof yet piston-movable). Answers rotate across mobs, blocks, items, foods, enchantments, and redstone.

questions.q: When you kill it, it splits into smaller copies of itself instead of just dying. The slime does this in the Overworld. Which mob does the same in the Nether?
questions.detail: Magma cubes split into smaller cubes when killed, just like slimes — the Nether counterpart.
questions.why: Players reason 'splitting = slime,' then must recall the lava-themed twin in the Nether.
questions.more: Tiny magma cubes drop nothing.

questions.q: The Nether is famous for hostile mobs, but exactly one mob that spawns there naturally is fully passive. Which one?
questions.detail: In Java Edition the strider is the only passive Nether mob; it walks on lava and can be saddled.
questions.why: People guess hoglin or zombified piglin, but those are hostile/neutral.
questions.more: Striders shiver and turn purple when they get cold off lava.

questions.q: Most mobs spawn based on darkness or biome. One flying mob only appears if the player goes too long without sleeping. Which one?
questions.detail: Phantoms spawn only after a player hasn't slept or died for three in-game days.
questions.why: Of the few flying mobs, only one is tied to the sleep mechanic.
questions.more: The gamerule doInsomnia false disables them.

questions.q: Some of these underwater zombies hurl a spinning weapon at you from a distance. Killing one is the only way to obtain that weapon. Which mob?
questions.detail: Drowned can spawn holding a trident and are the only source of tridents.
questions.why: The 'underwater zombie that throws' clue narrows to drowned.
questions.more: Only ~6.25% spawn with a trident in Java.

questions.cat: Minecraft · spawns from a thrown item
questions.q: This tiny bug-like mob is the only one that can spawn from a thrown ender pearl landing, and it disappears on its own after two minutes. Name it.
questions.detail: A thrown ender pearl has a 5% chance to spawn an endermite, which despawns within 2 minutes.
questions.why: Easy to confuse with silverfish; the ender-pearl trigger is the slicer.
questions.more: Endermen attack endermites.

questions.q: Creepers flee from one kind of mob, keeping their distance instead of attacking. Which tameable mob scares them?
questions.detail: Creepers (and ghasts) avoid cats and ocelots, staying about 6 blocks away.
questions.why: The set of mobs that repel another mob is tiny.
questions.more: Untamed jungle ocelots trigger the same fleeing.

questions.cat: Minecraft · killed by its own attack
questions.q: This large floating Nether mob shoots explosive fireballs. You can punch the fireball back and kill the mob with its own shot. Which one?
questions.detail: A deflected ghast fireball deals enough damage to one-shot the ghast.
questions.why: A deflectable straight-line projectile points to the ghast.
questions.more: A ghast killed by its own fireball drops the disc Tears.

questions.q: Strike this passive mushroom-biome animal with lightning and it changes color rather than dying or becoming a monster. Which mob?
questions.detail: A red mooshroom struck by lightning turns brown (and vice versa).
questions.why: Lightning usually turns pigs into zombified piglins; the mooshroom's color-swap is the reflex-defeater.
questions.more: Brown mooshrooms never spawn naturally.

questions.q: Bring this Nether mob into the Overworld and, after about 15 seconds, it transforms into an undead version of itself. Which mob?
questions.detail: A piglin left in the Overworld for ~15 seconds converts to a zombified piglin.
questions.why: The dimension-based timer plus 'wears gold, lives in Nether' narrows to piglin.
questions.more: Wearing gold armor stops piglins attacking you.

questions.q: Hostile mobs usually can't be bred, but this pig-like Nether beast can be fed crimson fungus to make babies. Which one?
questions.detail: Hoglins breed with crimson fungus — the only commonly-breedable hostile mob.
questions.why: Players assume no hostile mob breeds; the crimson-fungus clue points to the hoglin.
questions.more: Hoglins in the Overworld convert into zoglins.

questions.q: This hostile mob throws splash potions at you, but it also drinks potions itself to heal or resist damage during a fight. Which one?
questions.detail: Witches throw harmful splash potions and defensively drink healing, fire resistance, and swiftness potions.
questions.why: A mob that both throws and drinks potions is a unique combination.
questions.more: Witches have 85% magic resistance.

questions.cat: Minecraft · makes a creeper drop a disc
questions.dimension: makes a creeper drop a disc
questions.q: Creepers normally drop only gunpowder, but if a certain ranged mob lands the killing blow, the creeper drops a music disc. Which mob must do the killing?
questions.detail: When a skeleton (or stray) kills a creeper, it drops a random music disc.
questions.why: 'Ranged mob that fights creepers' plus the disc fact points to the skeleton.
questions.more: Pigstep and a few discs can't be obtained this way.

questions.q: Three mobs spawn naturally in the End. Two are the ender dragon and the enderman. Name the third — the box-shaped one that lives in End cities.
questions.detail: Endermen, the ender dragon, and shulkers are the only natural End mobs.
questions.why: Best-without phrasing removes the two easy answers, forcing the third.
questions.more: Shulkers are the only source of shulker shells.

questions.q: Villagers and piglins can open wooden doors, but on Hard difficulty one common undead mob will actually smash a wooden door to pieces. Which one?
questions.detail: On Hard difficulty a fraction of zombies can break wooden doors.
questions.why: The reflex 'mobs open doors' lists villagers; the slicer is 'breaks the door.'
questions.more: Only ~5% of zombies spawn with the ability.

questions.q: This powerful deep-dark mob has no working eyes at all — it finds you entirely by vibrations and smell, so sneaking can hide you from it. Which mob?
questions.detail: The warden is blind and detects players via vibration and smell; sneaking avoids detection.
questions.why: A mob defeated by sneaking must rely on sound — the warden's whole gimmick.
questions.more: Its ranged sonic boom pierces armor and shields.

questions.q: This tall neutral mob is so hurt by water that even rain damages it, making it teleport around frantically during a storm. Which mob?
questions.detail: Endermen take damage from water, including rain, and teleport to escape it.
questions.why: The 'tall + teleports from water' combo points uniquely to them.
questions.more: They turn hostile only if you look at their head.

questions.q: Hit one of these wild neutral animals and every other one of its kind nearby instantly turns on you as a pack. Which mob?
questions.detail: Attacking a wild wolf makes nearby wolves turn hostile and hunt as a pack.
questions.why: Among neutral/tameable mobs the wolf is the pack hunter.
questions.more: Tamed wolves never turn on their owner.

questions.cat: Minecraft · baby that never grows up
questions.q: This sped-up baby version of a common undead mob is more dangerous than the adult, yet it never grows up — it stays a baby forever. Which mob?
questions.detail: Baby zombies move faster, fit through 1-block gaps, and never mature.
questions.why: Players assume baby mobs grow up; the permanence is the reflex-defeater.
questions.more: They are the riders in chicken jockeys.

questions.q: This wall-climbing mob is hostile in the dark but goes neutral and ignores you once it stands in bright enough light. Which mob?
questions.detail: Spiders are hostile at light level 11 or below and neutral at 12 or above.
questions.why: 'Climbs walls + light-dependent' uniquely fits the spider.
questions.more: Spiders don't burn in daylight.

questions.q: This small stronghold pest hides inside stone-looking blocks, and attacking one can make a whole swarm burst out of the surrounding blocks. Which mob?
questions.detail: Damaging a silverfish that survives causes nearby silverfish to break out and swarm.
questions.why: Hiding-in-blocks plus chain-summon is unique; points to silverfish over endermite.
questions.more: A one-hit kill prevents the swarm.

questions.q: You can build this defender from blocks, but it also spawns by itself to guard villages and attacks nearly every hostile mob — except it leaves creepers alone. Which mob?
questions.detail: Iron golems are player-built or village-spawned and attack almost all hostiles, but ignore creepers to avoid explosions near villagers.
questions.why: Buildable + naturally village-spawning is a narrow combo (vs the snow golem).
questions.more: They hand poppies to villagers.

questions.q: Shear a sheep and it just loses its wool, but shear this rarer animal and it permanently turns into a plain cow. Which mob?
questions.detail: Shearing a mooshroom drops 5 mushrooms and permanently converts it into a normal cow.
questions.why: Shearing-changes-the-species is unique; contrast with the sheep reflex.
questions.more: Mooshrooms spawn only in mushroom fields.

questions.q: Every five to ten minutes this farm bird drops an item on the ground that you can pick up and throw. Which mob?
questions.detail: Adult chickens lay an egg every 5–10 minutes; the egg can be thrown and may hatch a chick.
questions.why: Egg-laying as a periodic drop is a single-mob behavior.
questions.more: Throwing eggs is a renewable way to start a chicken farm.

questions.q: Ranked by raw mining speed alone, which pickaxe material breaks blocks fastest, beating even netherite?
questions.detail: A golden pickaxe has the highest mining-speed multiplier but only ~33 durability.
questions.why: The obvious bet (netherite) is wrong; the trap is equating tier rank with speed.
questions.more: It can't even mine obsidian or diamond ore.

questions.cat: Minecraft · ore that drops no XP
questions.q: Diamond and coal ore give you experience when mined. Name an ore that gives none because what it drops still has to be smelted.
questions.detail: Iron (and gold) ore drop raw chunks meant for a furnace, and smeltable ores award XP at the furnace.
questions.why: Reason: 'which ore makes me smelt before use?' rather than memorize a table.
questions.more: Gold ore behaves the same way.

questions.q: A lava bucket smelts the most items of any single fuel. Setting it aside, which single fuel item smelts the most?
questions.detail: A block of coal smelts 80 items, beating a dried kelp block (20) and a blaze rod (12).
questions.why: Excluding the obvious winner forces a real comparison.
questions.more: A block smelts more than its 9 separate coal would.

questions.q: Which farmable, stackable food gives the most saturation, beating steak and cooked pork?
questions.detail: A golden carrot restores 6 hunger but 14.4 saturation, the highest of any normal food.
questions.why: The bet between 'steak' and 'golden carrot' is the game; the carrot wins on saturation.
questions.more: It ties the enchanted golden apple but is far cheaper.

questions.q: On a bow, Mending plays nicely with most enchantments but flatly refuses one. Which is it?
questions.detail: Infinity and Mending are mutually exclusive on bows.
questions.why: Reason from balance: unlimited arrows plus a self-repairing bow was judged overpowered.
questions.more: They were briefly combinable in 2015.

questions.q: Which boot enchantment lets you walk across open water by freezing it, and can't be made at an enchanting table?
questions.detail: Frost Walker turns nearby water into frosted ice; it's treasure-only and conflicts with Depth Strider.
questions.why: The 'walk on water' clue narrows the ~40 set to one.
questions.more: The ice melts shortly after you step off.

questions.q: Mine this storage block without Silk Touch and you get a pile of obsidian instead of the block. What block?
questions.detail: An ender chest drops 8 obsidian when broken normally; only Silk Touch returns the chest.
questions.why: The 'gives obsidian back' clue singles it out.
questions.more: All ender chests share one personal inventory.

questions.q: Which block survives TNT just like obsidian, yet unlike obsidian can still be shoved by a piston?
questions.detail: Ancient debris has obsidian-level blast resistance (1200) but pistons can move it.
questions.why: Two clues (TNT-proof + piston-movable) cross to a single answer.
questions.more: That makes it a niche redstone block.

questions.q: Which rare biome never naturally spawns hostile mobs, even in full darkness?
questions.detail: Mushroom fields suppress all natural hostile spawns — the only overworld biome that does.
questions.why: Recall narrows to rare biomes; the 'no monsters' rule pins one.
questions.more: It's the natural home of mooshrooms.

questions.q: Most planks catch fire near lava. Which two plank types, grown from giant Nether fungi, never burn?
questions.detail: Crimson and warped stems and planks are non-flammable, unlike every overworld wood.
questions.why: Reason from source: which 'woods' aren't really trees? The fireproof Nether fungi.
questions.more: They also can't be used as furnace fuel.

questions.q: Which simple, farmable drink instantly removes every status effect, good or bad, when you drink it?
questions.detail: Drinking milk clears all current status effects at once.
questions.why: The 'removes good and bad alike' framing makes the answer clear.
questions.more: Goats and mooshrooms also fill milk buckets.

questions.q: Which food fills almost no hunger and hits you with Poison, Hunger, and Nausea all at the same time?
questions.detail: Eating pufferfish gives ~0.5 hunger plus three bad effects at once.
questions.why: The triple-effect slicer is specific to one item.
questions.more: It's the brewing ingredient for Water Breathing.

questions.q: Which brewing ingredient corrupts a finished potion into a negative or opposite version, rather than adding a normal effect?
questions.detail: It turns potions into harmful or inverse forms (Healing to Harming, Night Vision to Invisibility).
questions.why: Reason from role: which ingredient is the 'twist' rather than the 'add'?
questions.more: It's crafted from a spider eye, sugar, and a brown mushroom.

questions.q: Which redstone component can lock its output in place when powered from the side, acting as a built-in latch?
questions.detail: A repeater powered from the side locks its output until unpowered.
questions.why: The 'can be locked / latch' property belongs to one component.
questions.more: It also adds adjustable delay and prevents backflow.

questions.q: Which redstone component can subtract one signal from another and read how full a chest is?
questions.detail: A comparator outputs main-minus-side in subtraction mode and reads container fullness 0–15.
questions.why: Only one part does arithmetic and container-reading.
questions.more: Right-click toggles compare and subtract modes.

questions.q: Which block both hurts mobs that brush it and instantly destroys any item dropped onto it?
questions.detail: A cactus damages adjacent mobs and deletes items that contact it.
questions.why: The dual 'damages mobs + deletes items' clue points to one plant.
questions.more: It breaks if a solid block is placed next to it.

questions.q: Which is the only flower that harms players and mobs, inflicting the Wither effect on anything standing on it?
questions.detail: A wither rose applies Wither to anything touching it; it appears when the Wither kills a mob.
questions.why: Among ~two dozen flowers, only one does damage.
questions.more: It can be planted in netherrack and soul soil.

questions.q: Which treasure-only enchantment goes on leggings (not boots) and makes you sneak faster?
questions.detail: Swift Sneak is a leggings enchantment found in ancient city loot, not the table.
questions.why: The 'leggings + sneak speed' pair is unique, defeating the boots reflex.
questions.more: At level III you sneak nearly as fast as you walk.

questions.q: One of Minecraft's two curses keeps a worn item stuck to you so you can't take it off. Which curse?
questions.detail: Curse of Binding prevents removing an equipped item except by dying or letting it break.
questions.why: Two curses exist; the 'can't take it off' behavior fingerprints one.
questions.more: The other curse, Vanishing, deletes the item on death.

questions.q: Which mining enchantment reaches level V, higher than Fortune or Unbreaking, to make tools dig faster?
questions.detail: Efficiency caps at V, while Fortune and Unbreaking stop at III.
questions.why: The max-level slicer separates Efficiency from its level-III neighbors.
questions.more: Efficiency V plus Haste can insta-mine many blocks.

questions.q: Which sword enchantment only boosts damage to spiders and similar bugs, and also briefly slows them?
questions.detail: It adds damage versus arthropods and inflicts Slowness on them.
questions.why: Reason from target group plus the unique slowness effect.
questions.more: It conflicts with Sharpness and Smite.

questions.q: Which trident enchantment makes a thrown trident return to your hand, and so can't share the weapon with Riptide?
questions.detail: Loyalty returns a thrown trident; it's mutually exclusive with Riptide.
questions.why: The return-to-hand behavior plus the Riptide conflict isolates one enchant.
questions.more: Channeling also can't coexist with Riptide.

questions.q: Which End fruit can be eaten even with a full hunger bar and randomly teleports you when you do?
questions.detail: Chorus fruit is edible at full hunger and teleports the eater a few blocks.
questions.why: The teleport side effect is unique to one food.
questions.more: Cooking it makes popped chorus fruit for purpur blocks.

questions.q: Which helmet enchantment lets you mine at normal speed underwater instead of the usual slowdown?
questions.detail: Aqua Affinity cancels the 5x underwater mining penalty; separate from Respiration.
questions.why: Two helmet water-enchants exist; the 'mining speed' clue picks the right one.
questions.more: It also works on a turtle shell helmet.

questions.q: Looting boosts mob drops. Which mining enchantment is its counterpart, multiplying drops from ores like diamond and lapis?
questions.detail: Fortune multiplies drops from ores that drop items directly; it doesn't help raw iron/gold.
questions.why: Reasoning by analogy to Looting plus the 'block drops' clue.
questions.more: It's mutually exclusive with Silk Touch.

questions.q: Which block automatically pulls items from the container above it and pushes them into the one it faces?
questions.detail: A hopper transfers items from the inventory above into the container it points toward.
questions.why: The directional auto-transfer behavior identifies one block.
questions.more: A comparator reads its fill level 0–15.

questions.cat: Minecraft · curse that deletes on death
questions.q: Which curse destroys the item completely when you die, so it never drops as loot?
questions.detail: Curse of Vanishing makes the item disappear on the holder's death.
questions.why: Paired against Binding, the 'deletes on death' behavior fingerprints the other curse.
questions.more: Often used on map-maker items.
